new_classes.label_en: PGI wine in containers > 2 l and ≤ 10 l (CN 2204 22 79)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit g.g.A. in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von mehr als 2 l bis 10 l (KN 2204 22 79)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes bearing a protected geographical indication (PGI/g.g.A.) presented in containers holding more than 2 litres and not more than 10 litres, classified under CN code 2204 22 79. Corresponds to the 22042200 subheading group (> 2 l and ≤ 10 l) with PGI quality status.

coverage_explanation: The two primary discriminating criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) the wine bears a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI/g.g.A.) — modelled via the existing eucn:PGIWineQuality quality class; (2) the product is presented in containers holding more than 2 litres and not exceeding 10 litres — modelled via the existing eucn:MediumContainer2To10L class. The parent class eucn:WineFreshGrapesGrapeMust2204 provides the necessary constraint that it is wine of fresh grapes under heading 2204. The legal text also references Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, which defines PGI wine more precisely (e.g. specific alcohol content, total dry extract thresholds). These detailed definitional criteria from Additional Note 6(a) are not yet represented as separate quality restrictions in the TBox, so coverage is slightly below 1.0. The sibling code 22042280 covers a related PGI wine category under the same container group; disjointness between these two PGI sibling codes cannot be fully axiomatised without knowing the further discriminating criterion (likely actual alcoholic strength) that separates 22042279 from 22042280, hence a small coverage gap remains.
